first-release.comment: The modern Win+H 'Voice Typing' feature shipped with Windows 11 in October 2021, renaming/replacing the older Windows 10 Dictation (which itself shipped with the Windows 10 Fall Creators Update in 2017). Using the Windows 11 introduction of the current Voice Typing feature per RESEARCH.md guidance for built-ins.

status.comment: Actively shipped and maintained as a core Windows 11 feature.

license.comment: Bundled, closed-source feature of the Windows operating system.

primary-type.comment: Real-time speak-to-type input method; not a file/meeting transcriber.

real-time-dictation.comment: Live speak-to-type via Win+H; words appear in the focused text box as you talk, with optional automatic punctuation.

file-transcription.comment: Voice Typing only handles live microphone input into a text field; it cannot import or transcribe existing audio/video files.

live-meeting-capture.comment: No meeting bot or system-audio capture; microphone-only dictation.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Produces a running transcript of your speech in near-real-time as you dictate.

audio-sources.comment: Microphone only; no file upload, system-audio, meeting-bot, or URL import.

underlying-engine.comment: Microsoft documents that Voice Typing uses online speech recognition powered by Azure Speech services. On Copilot+ PCs, an on-device 'Fluid dictation' small language model handles grammar/punctuation corrections.

language-count.comment: Approx. Microsoft describes Voice Typing in Windows 11 as supporting 50+ languages/dialects (multiple English locales plus French, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, etc.). Note: Voice Access (the separate hands-free control feature) supports far fewer. Checked 2026-06-12.

english-accuracy.comment: No published WER. Reviews generally rate Windows Voice Typing (cloud Azure recognition) as solid and reliable on clear English speech but error-prone on accents, technical vocabulary, and long-form vs Whisper-based tools. Rated 4 as a defensible mid-high estimate; no formal independent benchmark found.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: The dictation language is tied to the selected input language; there is no automatic mixed-language auto-detection within a session.

custom-vocabulary.comment: No user-facing custom dictionary or vocabulary-boosting list for Voice Typing. Windows' separate 'inking & typing personalization' builds a personal word list from your text input, but that is not a dictation custom-vocabulary mechanism.

timestamps.comment: Inserts plain text into the focused field; no timestamps.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Voice Typing only produces text; no summaries or notes.

built-in-editor.comment: Text is typed directly into whatever app has focus; there is no dedicated transcript editor.

translation.comment: Voice Typing transcribes speech; it does not translate the resulting text.

export-formats.comment: No transcript export; dictated text simply lands in the active text field of any app. No standalone document is produced to export.

processing-location.comment: Voice Typing uses online speech recognition powered by Azure Speech services and requires an internet connection; audio is sent to Microsoft servers. (The separate legacy 'Windows Speech Recognition' and Copilot+ 'Fluid dictation' offer some on-device processing, but standard Win+H Voice Typing is cloud-based.)

offline-capable.comment: Microsoft states Voice Typing requires an internet connection because it uses online Azure Speech services. Unlike Apple Dictation, standard Voice Typing does not run offline on typical PCs.

data-used-for-training.comment: Microsoft states it does not store, sample, or listen to voice recordings without permission. Contributing voice clips to improve Microsoft speech services is voluntary/opt-in via voice settings; work or school accounts cannot share voice data.

data-deletion-control.comment: Users can disable online speech recognition and clear their personal word list via Settings > Privacy & security > Speech and Inking & typing personalization; voice clip contributions can be turned off.

platforms.comment: Built into Windows 11 (Voice Typing). Windows 10 has the older Dictation under the same Win+H shortcut. Not available on macOS, iOS, Android, web, or Linux.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Win+H launches Voice Typing into any focused text box across the OS.

integrations.comment: No meeting/app integrations; it works as a system input method, not via Zoom/Meet/Teams/Slack connectors.

api-automation.comment: Voice Typing exposes no user-facing API or CLI. (Microsoft's separate Azure Speech SDK is a developer API but is not the Voice Typing feature.)

pricing-model.comment: Included free with Windows; no subscription or purchase.

free-tier.comment: Entirely free as a built-in OS feature.

free-allowance.comment: No usage caps; free with the operating system (requires internet).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free built-in feature covering full use; no paid tier.
